pattern: Because XOR is its own inverse, a prefix-XOR table lets any range be answered by combining two endpoints, turning each query from linear into constant time.
hints: Notice that xoring a value in and then xoring it again cancels it out completely.
Precompute a running XOR of every prefix; the XOR of a range is then the prefix at the right end combined with the prefix just before the left end.
Let pre[i] be the XOR of the first i elements. The answer for [l, r] is pre[r + 1] xor pre[l]. Build pre once, then answer all queries in constant time each.

edge_inputs.description: the worked example with overlapping ranges
single element queried as a full range
single-element ranges must return the element itself, not zero
two equal-bit values cancel to zero over their range
large values near the value cap

stress.description: large array and many random ranges to stress prefix reuse
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 30000))
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    q = max(1, min(size, 30000))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        a = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        b = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if a > b:
            a, b = b, a
        queries.append([a, b])
    return [arr, queries]
stress.note: every left <= right and indices in bounds by construction
